Name: Tommy Tremble
Outlook: Quick, name the best TE on the Carolina Panthers roster. Exactly! Name: Max Klare
Outlook: Before transferring to Ohio State for his final year, Klare was a production monster at Purdue and showcased target-earning ability that could intrigue at the next level. Unfortunately, his first-year prospects are dim as he was selected by the Rams, who collect TEs like it is going out of style. Los Angeles has Colby Parkinson, Tyler Higbee, Terrance Ferguson, and Davis Allen on the roster, making the rookie just another guy in the room. Sean McVay has shown a strong proclivity to running 13-personnel, so we know Klare will be rotated in.

Name: Jake Tonges
Outlook: For a few weeks in 2025, “Tonges McBasketball” was the hero we didn’t know we needed. With George Kittle nursing his hamstring from Week 2 through Week 6, Tonges stepped up valiantly, giving us at least a couple start-worthy weeks. He had three weeks on the season as the TE9 or better. With Kittle battling back from his Achilles injury, there is a strong chance he won’t be ready to start the year or resemble the same old Kittle when he does. Tonges is practically free in drafts and will be the assumed TE1 on a Shanahan-led team. Consider him a low investment if you need to plug the TE position early.

Name: Oscar Delp
Outlook: There was a lot of chatter around what could have been with Oscar Delp, but looking at his numbers from college, he never really delivered. He never hit 300 receiving yards at Georgia thanks to competition with some guy named Brock Bowers basically running the offense during Delp’s first two seasons. Even with questions on the production profile, the Saints grabbed him in the third round to add youth to their TE room. He will have a tough time making an impact in redraft this year, presumably buried on the TE depth chart behind Juwan Johnson and Noah Fant.
